TextENGNew: "Ummm...@k@vS08/13/321000993. We're supposed to follow this road, then..."
"Oh, Papa.@k@vS08/34/321500065. Isn't that a map over there?"
Miyuki pointed to a local directory as she said that.
I compared the map with the directions written in the invitation, and determined that we would make it there if we just walked a little farther.
"Yaaay, snow, snooow~.♪"
I guess Miyuki hasn't seen snowfall this thick very often. She's been playing in it since we left the station.
This town didn't have many people coming and going, so the snow along the roadside was still fairly clean. It was a comfortable walk, and I left a neat trail of footprints behind me as I went.
"Come on, Miyuki.@k@vS08/13/321000995. It's dangerous to run around too much, you know?"
"I'll be okaaaay.♪@k@vS08/34/321500068. ...Wah?"
Miyuki answered vigorously, but she lost her footing shortly after and tumbled to the ground.

...Thankfully, she was wearing her raincoat. And since the ground was covered in soft snow, I didn't have to worry about her getting seriously hurt. But considering where we were headed, it didn't seem like proper behavior.
"Eehehee~, so coooold.♪@k@vS08/34/321500070. Isn't snow interesting, Papa?"
"...Interesting?@k@vS08/13/321000997. Hahaha, I guess so."
"Really, you're the one that makes the snow interesting, Miyuki." ...I suddenly muttered in my head.
Honestly... just looking at her smiling face warmed my heart.
If I didn't have Miyuki in my life, how much worse would my life be?
"How about we go skiing next time we're on vacation?@k@vS08/13/321000999. Yeah, how about we go to Hokkaido?@k@vS08/13/321001000. I'm sure Ooishi-san will be pleased to see you."
"Old man Ooishi?@k@vS08/34/321500072. Yeah, I wanna see him too!@k@vS08/34/321500073. I'm looking forward to it, Papa!"
But considering my tireless work schedule, I had no idea when I'd be able to fulfill that promise. ...Still, I made a promise to Miyuki, so I'd have to do my best to follow through.
Recently, she'd stopped asking "when?" about these sorts of topics.
Apparently, my in-laws told her, "It's causes trouble when you ask that."
She's a really smart, really kind girl... and I'm disappointed that I have to spend so much time away from her.
After walking for a while, we reached a larger road and were able to see a wider area around us. Then, I spotted a small church off in the direction we were headed.
Seeing the building covered in white snow gave it a majestic atmosphere, and gave me a pious feeling.
...Someone once told me that snow was the breath of God, gathering all the world's sorrows into Heaven, then falling from the sky.
So, this weather was proof that God had forgiven her sin... I wanted to believe that.
"...Oh, Akasaka-san!@k@vS08/37/320300497. Over here, over here!"
I turned toward the source of that voice and noticed two women standing in front of the gate, waving vigorously in my direction.
For a moment, I wasn't sure who they were... but as I got closer, it all came back to me, and I couldn't help but smile.
"Long time no see, Akasaka-san!@k@vS08/37/320300499. Thank you for coming."
"It's been a while, Saeki-san.@k@vS08/13/321001004. ...Oh, is it still alright for me to call you "Saeki" now?"
"Sure, whichever.@k@vS08/37/320300501. I'd even be ok if you called me Elizabeth or Antoinette in private, alright?"
"...You're aware that those are first names, not last names, right?"
"Ahahahaha."
"Thank you very much for coming all the way out here today.@k@vS08/38/320400157. I was a little worried that the trains would stop running due to the snow."
"Yeah, leaving early was the right call.@k@vS08/13/321001007. ...Oh, even though I'm a little late in saying this, thank you for the invitation."
"Thank you very much~.♪"
Miyuki followed my lead and bowed her head as she said that. When the two of them saw that, they shouted "cuuute~☆" in a shrill voice and crouched down to start talking to my daughter.
"...Oh, would it be alright to see our two guests of honor?"
"Yeah, that should be fine.@k@vS08/37/320300503. The groom is busy running errands at the moment, so you should go see the bride first."
"Understood. Uh..."
"Aah, we can watch your little girl for you.@k@vS08/38/320400159. ...Uh, can you tell me your name?"
"Umm... I'm Miyuki Akasaka."
"Ahahaha, nicely done.♪@k@vS08/38/320400161. Well Miyuki-chan, do you want to go make a snowman with us?"
"Yaaay.♪"
After that exchange, the two of them walked to the garden with Miyuki.
...I thanked the two of them for taking care of her, then made my way to the church.
"...Come in."
I heard a gentle voice and a bell from inside the room beckoning me, so I opened the door.
...She was sitting alone by the windowsill, but as soon as she saw my face she stood up with a bright smile.
"...Oh, Akasaka-san!@k@vS08/36/320100640. So you were able to come after all?"
"Yes, sorry for not keeping in touch.@k@vS08/13/321001011. Thank you for inviting me here today.@k@vS08/13/321001012. ...No, I guess I should be saying congratulations?"
"...Ahah, thank you very much."
Then, she pointed to one of the empty chairs and said, "Please have a seat."
"I think Akira-kun will be back soon.@k@vS08/36/320100643. He wanted to see you too, Akasaka-san."

"I'm glad to hear that.@k@vS08/13/321001014. ...Unfortunately, Ooishi-san is feeling a little under the weather.@k@vS08/13/321001015. He's really sorry about that.@k@vS08/13/321001016. But he asked me to give you all his best regards."
"I see.@k@vS08/36/320100645. ...That's unfortunate, but I suppose it can't be helped.@k@vS08/36/320100646. I'll send him a letter later."
Suddenly... Natsumi-san and I turned our eyes out the window at the same time and enjoyed the snowy scenery.
From here, we could see Saeki-san and Tamako-san playing with Miyuki in the garden.
"...Is that your daughter?"
"Yes.@k@vS08/13/321001019. I'm sorry, I know she wasn't on the invitation... but she really wanted to come and wouldn't take no for an answer."
"Ahahaha, don't worry, I don't mind at all.@k@vS08/36/320100650. ...There aren't a lot of people here that would."
"...So, who else is here besides me?"
"Chisato-chan and Tamako-chan are attending.@k@vS08/36/320100652. Aside from that... nobody.@k@vS08/36/320100653. Akira-kun's parents are also showing him consideration by not attending..."
"...I see."
I managed to understand what she was implying without asking any other details.

Normally, the groom calls on the relatives to unite the family. ...However, there was nobody left in the bride's immediate family. And the groom's relatives were probably already familiar with the bride's poor public reputation.
And of course, it would be difficult for the bride to call on her distant relatives. Because anyone who came... would be from Hinamizawa.

"Akira-kun and his family will never say it... but I know.@k@vS08/36/320100655. Before Akira-kun introduced me to his parents in advance of the ceremony today... he got in a fight with them so bad they nearly cut ties with him altogether."
"It took a long time to persuade them.@k@vS08/36/320100657. And not just Akira-kun. Chisato-chan and Tamako-chan have been desperately running around and helping out..."
"...I see."
...About five years have passed since the incident.
Natsumi Kimiyoshi voluntarily came to Kakiuchi Station with her friends and turned herself in.
In the subsequent trial, the results of her psychiatric examination came out... A doctor submitted a report stating that her judgment was impaired by her mental illness.
Thankfully, they were able to make great use of all the work Chief Inspector Minai put into researching her medication, so they were able to convince the prosecutors of her mental condition.
Plus, her friends offered testimony in her defense. As a result, Natsumi Kimiyoshi was declared "not guilty" in the eyes of the court...
Even so, that by no means meant everything went back to normal.

The fact that she committed murder and tried to hide it was permanently attached to Natsumi Kimiyoshi's reputation. It became impossible to live in her old town, so she moved to a distant, secluded town.
And... fortunately, she didn't move there alone...

After that, Natsumi Kimiyoshi was admitted to a psychiatric hospital and given treatment so intense it was like being bled... The counseling, medication, and rehabilitation continued for several years.
It goes without saying... she received a lot of support from her two friends, and the groom Akira Toudou.
I'm sure it wasn't smooth sailing. ...I can imagine all the hardship she must have suffered over the years.
But even so, she did her best.
And finally, she managed to meet him like this today...
"Up until a short time ago... all I ever thought about was dying."

"Killing my family... making a mess of the lives of my friends and the man I loved...@k@vS08/36/320100660. I questioned if I truly deserved to be alive.@k@vS08/36/320100661. But more than that... I felt I had no right to be happy..."
I quickly noticed my error and suddenly stopped myself. Then,
"...Natsumi Toudou.@k@vS08/36/320100663. That name... might take a lifetime to get used to.@k@vS08/36/320100664. However..."
Then, Natsumi Kimiyo... Toudou raised her head to look up at me.
I could still see the grief and doubt in her expression... but even so, she smiled.
At least, she tried to smile...
"...I can't die."
"Because there are people out there who care so much about me...@k@vS08/36/320100667. So, I can't choose to take the easy path... absolutely not.@k@vS08/36/320100668. I don't think that's an option."
"So... I don't care if I'll never be any happier than this.@k@vS08/36/320100670. Because I'm already happy right now."
"And for the people who care about me... I'd like to do what I can to make them happy.@k@vS08/36/320100672. Right now... that's what I'm thinking about."
".......@k@vS08/13/321001029. Yes, I think that sounds good.@k@vS08/13/321001030. You... should continue to live your life."
"...Thank you."
As she listened to my words, Natsumi wiped the tears from her eyes. Then,
"...I wanted to tell her."
"I wanted to tell the detective who encouraged me so much... how I feel right now.@k@vS08/36/320100676. That... is my greatest regret."
Later, a small ceremony was conducted in the chapel.
The priest held the Bible in his hand as he delivered his speech.
The only people watching were her two friends, my daughter, and I.
Thinking about it that way, it seemed like a lonely affair. ...Even so, I felt fortunate to be in attendance, as I could feel the happiness in the air clinging to my entire body.
"...Now, please exchange the rings and say your vows."
After the priest's declaration, the groom looked nervous as he pulled out the ring. Then, he slid it onto her ring finger as he swore to spend the rest of his life with her.
Natsumi gazed into him in that moment.
Was she afraid?
Was she sad?
She trembled in embarrassment, guilt and excitement as she faintly began moving her mouth to speak.
"...Akira-kun..."
Still... Natsumi pushed herself to smile.
For her lover who gently embraced her as she was.
And for her friends who took her hand and always had her back.
For the people who encouraged and supported her... she flashed them a genuine smile.
"Her world has changed... It's still very cruel, but..."
"Huh?"
"...There is still hope. I'm certain of that..."
Miyuki was staring up at me with a vacant expression.
...I guess a day will come when I have to see this girl off, too.
When that day comes... I hope I can send her off with a smile.
And when that day comes, I hope my daughter will have enough memories of happy days to be able to smile like this from the bottom of her heart.
Yes, that's what I decided, as the warm sentiment of the two people in front of me carried through to me...
